factors.name: US-Iran Peace Deal (Geopolitical Risk-Off)
factors.summary: Trump announced ceasefire agreement with Iran; Strait of Hormuz reopening set for June 19, eliminating 4-month war premium and supply shock. Oil down >4% on deal (WTI/Brent heavy selling), reducing energy input costs for Dow industrials and transportation stocks.

factors.name: Oil Price Decline
factors.summary: Crude down 3-4% as Hormuz reopening removes geopolitical premium and Iranian sanctions eased. Lower energy costs benefit CVX dividend, reduce inflation/input pressures for CAT, BA, transportation, and improve real yields backdrop.

factors.name: Equity Futures Rally on Deal News
factors.summary: ES and NQ up 0.7% and 1.0% respectively in Globex after Iran deal announcement (June 14 22:03 UTC). Dow futures likely tracking higher; positive open expected Monday June 16.

factors.name: USD Weakness
factors.summary: Dollar marked lower in early Asia trade on peace news. While this can weigh short-term on USD-based equities translation, it reflects risk-on sentiment and lower safe-haven demand—net bullish for risk assets like equities.

factors.name: Fed Policy / Kevin Warsh Uncertainty
factors.summary: Kevin Warsh takes helm as new Fed Chair this week; MarketWatch notes economists don't know what to expect. FOMC meeting and press conference upcoming (likely Tue/Wed)—macro significance high but direction TBD pending guidance.

factors.name: Rate Expectations / Long-End Yields
factors.summary: No major inflation print or Fed surprise yet in news window. Lower oil (disinflationary) may ease rate-hike fears, supporting dividend-heavy Dow names (UNH, JPM, GS), but Warsh presser could shift expectations sharply.

factors.name: Dow Heavyweight Earnings Outlook
factors.summary: No major Dow component earnings reported in news window. Earnings season typically in April/May; June is quiet. Forward guidance from industrials (CAT, BA, HON) will be key post-deal narrative (improved order outlook on stabilized global backdrop).

factors.name: China Growth / Trade Data
factors.summary: No recent PMI or trade releases in news. However, Iran deal + oil decline + potential Fed dovish lean could improve EM/China appetite; Caterpillar and Boeing could see demand boost if global manufacturing inflection seen.

factors.name: Risk-Off Sentiment Reversal (Knicks Celebration / Civil Unrest Minor)
factors.summary: NYC Knicks championship triggered some celebratory disorder; 63 arrests, 1 teen shot. Economically immaterial but suggests retail risk appetite intact. Not a material macro driver for Dow.

factors.name: G7 Summit / Trump Tariff Narrative
factors.summary: Trump attending G7 in France this week after UFC event; agenda includes Ukraine, Iran, trade. Tariff threats remain latent but peace deal may delay escalation near-term. No new tariff news in window.

key_levels.support: 51,000 (4h MA zone), 50,700 (recent swing low before deal)

screening.narrative: The US-Iran peace deal announced late Sunday (June 14) was the dominant catalyst: oil slumped 3-4% on Hormuz reopening expectations, equity futures rallied 0.7-1%, and safe-haven demand (USD, duration) weakened. Dow futures up in sympathy, with industrial and energy names (CAT, CVX, BA, GS, JPM) set to benefit from lower input costs, reduced geopolitical premium, and improved global trade backdrop. However, the move is already underway in US30 (+0.30% 24h) and equity futures are bid, placing this in mid-Phase 2; real catalysts ahead include Warsh's first FOMC presser (mid-week) and any new headlines on Iran deal signing (set June 19). Risk: escalation from Israel/Iran (Netanyahu not pleased with Trump's pushback) or Warsh hawkishness could quickly reverse sentiment.
catalysts_ahead: Kevin Warsh FOMC Meeting & Press Conference (Tue/Wed June 17-18) — critical macro reset
US-Iran Deal Formal Signing (Friday June 19) — execution risk / renegotiation headlines
Potential Israel-Iran escalation (ongoing medium-term risk; Iran 'warned' strikes could resume)
Fed Fund Futures repricing into Warsh presser
ISM Manufacturing & Services PMI (if released early week) — Dow industrial health check
Dow component earnings guidance / conference calls (forward guidance on tariffs, global demand)
Oil price stability (watch for Iran oil re-entry timing and geopolitical spikes)
screening.risk_warning: Move is already underway (+0.30% 24h); if Warsh signals hawkish stance or tightens guidance, Dow could reverse sharply lower, especially if long-duration bonds (high-dividend Dow names like JNJ, UNH, MCD) reprice. Escalation from Israel-Iran (Netanyahu reportedly unhappy) or new tariff threats from Trump at G7 could also undercut rally. Wait for retest of support after initial enthusiasm fades.
screening.entry_guidance: Move underway in Phase 2; wait for pullback to 51,000–51,100 support (4h MA) or intraday retest of 51,200 after open before adding longs. Avoid chasing extended moves into FOMC presser (mid-week volatility likely). Short-term longs favor on dips with stop below 50,800.

confirmation.entry_guidance: Enter on minor dip or at market open; peace deal premium not yet fully priced in US regular session, futures up 0.7%.

factors.name: US-Iran Peace Deal
factors.summary: US and Iran reached interim agreement to reopen Strait of Hormuz and halt hostilities, reducing major geopolitical risk premium. Deal signing set for Friday June 19 in Switzerland. Trump confirmed Hormuz opening Friday upon signing.

factors.name: Oil Price Collapse
factors.summary: Oil slumped over 4% on Hormuz reopening news and Iranian supply returning to market within weeks. Lower energy input costs are bullish for Dow industrials (CAT, MMM, HON, BA) and reduce inflation pressures. CVX drag offset by broader margin benefit.

factors.name: Equity Futures Response
factors.summary: ES futures up 0.7%, NQ up 1.0% in Sunday Globex session on peace deal announcement. Risk-on sentiment clearly dominant; Dow futures participating in relief rally as geopolitical tail risk removed.

factors.name: Supply Chain Normalization
factors.summary: Strait of Hormuz reopening (20% of global oil passes through) ends months-long supply chain disruption. LNG tanker already heading toward Hormuz. Bullish for Dow multinationals with Asia/Europe exposure and trade-sensitive industrials.

factors.name: Dollar Weakness
factors.summary: USD marked lower in early Asia trading on peace news. Weaker dollar is positive for Dow's ~50% international revenue exposure, improves translation effects for multinationals (MSFT, AAPL, CAT, BA).

factors.name: European Sanctions Relief
factors.summary: UK, France, Germany, Italy ready to lift relevant Iran sanctions per joint statement. Broader de-escalation and normalization of trade flows supports global growth outlook and Dow cyclicals.

factors.name: Fed Meeting This Week
factors.summary: Kevin Warsh's first Fed meeting as chair this week creates uncertainty. WSJ notes 'economists honestly don't know what to expect.' Oil drop is disinflationary, potentially dovish signal, but Warsh policy stance unclear. Caps conviction until after FOMC.

factors.name: Geopolitical Tail Risk
factors.summary: Israel struck Beirut despite ceasefire talks; Iranian state TV threatened 'heavy bombing' of Israel tonight. Trump publicly rebuked Netanyahu as 'very difficult,' threatened to resume Iran strikes if nuclear accord fails. Deal fragility remains; 30-day Hormuz deadline and managed (not unconditional) reopening per MOU details.

factors.name: Price Action Context
factors.summary: Dow at 51,202, up only +0.30% in 24h, -0.07% in 4h. Move has NOT extended yet; peace news broke Sunday evening US time, markets were closed. This is fresh catalyst entering Monday session.

factors.name: Dow Sector Composition
factors.summary: Dow's industrial-heavy composition (CAT, BA, HON, MMM, GE) benefits directly from lower oil/input costs and supply chain normalization. Financials (JPM, GS, V) benefit from risk-on and lower recession risk. CVX is only 1 of 30 names.

confirmation.narrative: US-Iran peace deal announced Sunday evening represents major positive catalyst for Dow, removing geopolitical premium that has weighed on markets for months. Strait of Hormuz reopening (set for Friday) collapses oil prices 4%+, directly benefiting Dow's industrial base through lower input costs and restoring critical supply chain link. Equity futures gapped higher 0.7% in thin Sunday Globex trade, signaling strong Monday open likely. However, deal fragility remains—Israel-Lebanon tensions flaring, Trump threatening to resume strikes if nuclear talks fail within 60 days, and managed (not unconditional) Hormuz reopening creates execution risk. Fed meeting this week with new Chair Warsh adds uncertainty, though oil collapse is disinflationary and potentially dovish.
catalysts_ahead: FOMC meeting this week (Kevin Warsh's first as Fed Chair, policy stance unknown)
Iran peace deal signing Friday June 19 in Switzerland
Strait of Hormuz actual reopening Friday per Trump statement
Potential Iranian retaliation against Israel (state TV threatened 'heavy bombing tonight')
G7 summit this week in France (Trump attending, Iran deal likely focus)
confirmation.risk_warning: Deal collapses if Israel-Iran hostilities resume (Iranian state TV threatened strikes on Israel tonight), Trump resumes strikes, or Hormuz reopening delayed beyond Friday. Hawkish surprise from Warsh at FOMC would override oil drop benefits. European demand for 'unconditional' Hormuz access conflicts with MOU terms of 'managed' reopening—potential friction point.

reason: FRESH high-conf read (7/10) — informational only, needs confirmation

factors.name: US-Iran Peace Deal (Geopolitical Risk-Off)
factors.summary: US and Iran reached interim MoU to reopen Strait of Hormuz, ending ~4 months of conflict. Global risk sentiment sharply improved; Asian equities surged (SoftBank +12%, Taiwan +2.36%), while safe-haven flows reversed (USD weakened, DXY ~99.50).

factors.name: Oil Price Collapse
factors.summary: WTI tumbled to 2-month low near $79.40 on Hormuz reopening and supply normalization expectations. Lower energy costs benefit Dow's cyclical/industrial names and reduce input cost inflation pressures.

factors.name: US Dollar Weakness
factors.summary: DXY fell from elevated levels as safe-haven demand faded post-deal. Weaker USD lifts multinational earnings translation for Dow's ~50% international revenue exposure (particularly CAT, BA, GE, JPM).

factors.name: Rate-Cut Expectations Lift (Bonds Rally)
factors.summary: Treasury yields advanced across curve as Fed rate-hike odds trimmed post-Iran deal. Lower long-end yields support dividend-payers and rate-sensitive Dow heavyweights (JPM, GS, utilities exposure).

factors.name: Trade Policy Uncertainty (Trump Tariff Threats)
factors.summary: Trump threatened France with 100% wine tariffs over tech 'sales tax'; G7 critical minerals pricing plan faces skepticism. Tariff escalation could pressure margin-sensitive industrials (CAT, BA, MMM, HON) and cross-border supply chains.

factors.name: Execution Risk on Deal (MoU, Not Signed)
factors.summary: Deal scheduled to sign Friday (2026-06-20); Netanyahu says Israel not bound by Lebanon ceasefire clause; Hormuz reopening gradual, not immediate, with unspecified verification. Market euphoria carries execution/geopolitical tail risk.

factors.name: Dow Price Action (Near-Flat, Move Not Fully Priced)
factors.summary: US30 +0.30% 24h, -0.07% 4h. Asian/EU equities surged on deal news; US futures jumped overnight but Dow itself has not yet fully participated in rally. Room for catch-up in US cash session.

factors.name: Economic Data Thin (No Major Releases in Window)
factors.summary: No CPI, ISM, NFP, or FOMC in immediate 24h news window. German wholesale prices +5.9% YoY (inflation still sticky), Swedish unemployment 9.4% (mixed). Lack of US hard data reduces conviction.

factors.name: SpaceX Debut Sentiment (Risk-On Momentum)
factors.summary: SpaceX surged past $2T market cap on Nasdaq debut; Gina Rinehart made 'significant' SpaceX investment. Tech/growth euphoria from Friday carry over, benefiting Dow growth and financials (JPM, MSFT on Dow).

factors.name: ECB Cautious Optimism (Lagarde on Inflation)
factors.summary: ECB welcomes Iran deal's inflation relief but warns of second-round effects in services. European sticky inflation narrative intact; benefits US Dow if Fed can stay dovish longer.

screening.narrative: The US-Iran peace deal triggered a sharp global risk-on shift overnight: geopolitical premium collapsed, oil crashed ~6%, safe-haven flows unwound, and Asian/European equities rallied 2–12%. The Dow has not yet fully caught up to this euphoria in US cash trading, offering near-term participation potential for cyclical and multinational names (CAT, BA, JPM, GE) that benefit from lower oil costs and stronger foreign earnings translation. However, execution risk on the MoU (signing Friday, Israel's non-compliance on Lebanon, gradual Hormuz clearing) and Trump's renewed tariff threats (France 100% wine tariff, G7 minerals skepticism) create a 'sell-the-news' tail. Current price action (flat-to-+0.3%) suggests Phase 2 momentum building but not yet exhausted.
catalysts_ahead: US-Iran Peace Deal Signing (Friday, 2026-06-20) — execution risk and any last-minute geopolitical flare-up
BOJ Rate Hike to 1% (Tuesday, 2026-06-17) — yen weakness could extend risk-on rally globally
G7 Summit (Évian-les-Bains, this week) — Trump tariff and critical minerals negotiations in real-time
ISM Manufacturing PMI (late week) — first hard US economic data post-deal; sticky inflation vs. cooling cycle
US Treasury Auction Cycle (ongoing) — yield sensitivity for Dow dividend payers and rate-exposed financials
China Municipal Property Stimulus Rolls (ongoing) — 470+ measures announced; impacts CAT, BA, materials demand
screening.risk_warning: If Israel escalates in Lebanon or Iran signals non-compliance before Friday signing, geopolitical premium snaps back, oil rallies, and Dow's euphoria reverses. Tariff escalation (Trump-France, G7 breakdown) could overwhelm energy-cost relief and hit industrials hard. ECB inflation concerns + sticky service-sector pricing could force Fed to hold rates higher for longer, pressuring valuations. Gap-fill risk if overnight rally doesn't hold into US cash open.
screening.entry_guidance: Move is in early Phase 2 (2–4h old); wait for US cash open (09:30 ET) to confirm Dow participation in overnight rally. If US30 holds above 51100 at open, add on dips to 51000–51050. If momentum stalls or tariff headlines resurface, await retest of 51000 before re-entering.

factors.name: US-Iran Peace Deal (Geopolitical De-escalation)
factors.summary: US and Iran reached interim MOU to reopen Strait of Hormuz with 60-day nuclear negotiations window. Trump confirmed deal completion; Vance stated US has 'all the cards' in talks. This removes major tail risk that drove oil spike to $120+.

factors.name: Oil Price Collapse
factors.summary: WTI crude dropped below $80 (three-month low) and Brent retreating toward $80 per MUFG commentary. Lower energy input costs reduce inflation pressure on industrials and improve corporate margins—highly positive for Dow cyclicals (CAT, HON, BA).

factors.name: Treasury Yields Declining
factors.summary: 10-year UST yield fell 4bps to 4.441% as Iran deal pared Fed hike expectations (market now pricing 16bps tightening vs 24bps pre-deal). Lower rates support dividend-heavy Dow stocks (utilities, financial dividend payers) and reduce cost of capital.

factors.name: USD Weakness (Risk-On Sentiment)
factors.summary: Dollar trading lower across major pairs (EURUSD, USDJPY, GBPUSD) per DXY commentary. Weaker USD lifts valuations for Dow's ~50% international revenue exposure—positive translation effects for multinationals (MSFT, JPM, BA, CAT).

factors.name: Futures Surging Pre-Open
factors.summary: S&P 500 futures up 1.3% at 7:45 AM ET per Bloomberg (pre-market data). This broad risk-on mood extends to large-cap, price-weighted indices like the Dow.

factors.name: June Empire Fed Manufacturing PMI
factors.summary: US June Empire State manufacturing headline collapsed to +5.7 vs +14.0 expected and +19.6 prior (per InvestingLive). New orders +3.5 vs +22.7 prior; shipments +8.6 vs +18.9. Supply availability at -13.9 (lowest since June 2022) shows demand weakness despite rate-sensitive rebound.

factors.name: SpaceX IPO Momentum & Tech Strength
factors.summary: SpaceX gained 6% in premarket after IPO, now valued >$2T. Ron Baron bought $1B more, lifting stake to $25B. Semiconductor/defense space (relevant to Dow aerospace/defense) showing strength on defense spending backdrop.

factors.name: M&A Activity (Roku, Nuvei-Payoneer, AmEx-TheFork)
factors.summary: Fox acquiring Roku for $22B; Nuvei buying Payoneer for $2.75B; AmEx buying TheFork for $700M. Active M&A signals confidence in economic outlook and liquidity. Roku rallied to four-year high.

factors.name: Fed Chair Warsh Takes Helm (Policy Uncertainty)
factors.summary: New FOMC chair Kevin Warsh begins tenure this week. Market awaits his tone on rate cuts. Strategists (Citrini) suggest stock market could 'go crazy' if he signals dovish bias, but uncertainty remains ahead of FOMC meeting.

factors.name: China AI Competition (Zhipu surge)
factors.summary: Zhipu surged 33% after Anthropic curbs, but this reflects regulatory headwinds on US AI exports and potential margin compression for US chip leaders—mixed for Dow (MSFT exposure, but also broader tech risk).

screening.narrative: The Dow opened Monday riding a strong risk-on wave following the US-Iran peace agreement, which triggered a swift collapse in oil prices (WTI <$80), a 4bp drop in 10Y Treasury yields, and broad USD weakness. This combination is textbook bullish for the price-weighted Dow: lower energy input costs improve industrials margins, weaker dollar lifts multinational translation earnings, and declining rates support dividend-payers and reduce cost of capital. However, the June Empire Fed manufacturing PMI painted a stark contradictory picture—headline collapsed from +19.6 to +5.7, with new orders plummeting from +22.7 to +3.5, signaling demand weakness in the real economy despite the rally in rate expectations. The move has already begun (+0.30% in 24h, futures surged 1.3%); we are in Phase 2.
catalysts_ahead: FOMC decision & Warsh presser (late this week) — tone on rate cuts/hikes critical
ISM Manufacturing PMI (June) — forward read on industrial demand
Durable goods orders, factory orders — real cyclical demand check
Dow component earnings (UNH, GS, JPM, BA, CAT, HD) — earnings revision risk if demand softens
US-Iran formal signing in Switzerland (June 19) — geopolitical tail-risk closure
Oil inventory data & Strait of Hormuz reopening timeline — sustaining lower oil narrative
BoJ rate hike (this week) — currency/rates spillover to USD, equities
screening.risk_warning: Empire Fed weakness signals underlying demand deterioration; if ISM/durable goods confirm the manufacturing downturn, the rally may be premature risk-on exuberance rather than justified by earnings. Additionally, the move has already begun (Phase 2); if oil stabilizes above $85 or yields re-steepen on Warsh hawkishness, momentum reversal is likely. Geopolitical risks remain if Iran deal talks collapse or Israel rejects Lebanon ceasefire clause.
screening.entry_guidance: Move is already underway and extended in 4-hour timeframe (+0.30% 24h); avoid chasing at resistance 51400. Wait for a pullback to support 51000-51100 for a safer long entry, or monitor for FOMC/ISM data to confirm bullish thesis before adding to longs.

factors.name: US-Iran Peace Framework
factors.summary: US and Iran reached interim deal to reopen Strait of Hormuz, ending months-long war. Trump confirmed 'Deal with Islamic Republic of Iran is now complete.' Major geopolitical risk premium removed, historically bullish for equities.

factors.name: Oil Price Collapse
factors.summary: WTI crude dropped below $80 for first time since March, down ~35% from April peaks per multiple sources. Lower energy input costs directly benefit Dow industrials (CAT, MMM, HON) and reduce inflation pressures, supporting dovish Fed pivot.

factors.name: Fed Rate Hike Expectations
factors.summary: Market now pricing only 16 bps of Fed tightening by year-end vs 24 bps before Iran deal announcement per investinglive. Lower oil prices reducing inflation pressure argues against aggressive hiking, supportive for rate-sensitive equities.

factors.name: Treasury Yields
factors.summary: 10-year Treasury yield fell 4+ bps to 4.441% per CNBC, with bonds rallying on Iran deal. Lower yields reduce discount rates and benefit Dow's dividend payers and financials with rate-sensitive business lines.

factors.name: Risk Sentiment / Equity Futures
factors.summary: S&P 500 futures up 1.3% in premarket per Bloomberg. Broad risk-on environment with VIX likely declining, credit spreads tightening. Dow typically participates in broad equity rallies despite industrial weighting.

factors.name: Dollar Weakness
factors.summary: USD lower across the board on Iran deal per multiple FX sources. Weaker dollar benefits Dow's 50% international revenue exposure through favorable translation effects—heavier impact than SPY given multinational composition.

factors.name: M&A Activity
factors.summary: Major deals announced: Fox buying Roku for $22B, AmEx buying TheFork for $700M, Nuvei-Payoneer $2.75B, Salesforce-Fin $3.6B. Active M&A market signals corporate confidence and typically supports equity valuations.

factors.name: Empire Manufacturing PMI
factors.summary: June Empire Fed +5.7 vs +14.0 expected, down sharply from +19.6 prior. New orders collapsed to +3.5 from +22.7. Significant manufacturing slowdown directly relevant to Dow's industrial-heavy composition (CAT, MMM, HON, BA).

factors.name: SpaceX IPO Momentum
factors.summary: SpaceX rallied 6% in premarket after record debut, now valued over $2 trillion. Ron Baron bought $1B more shares. Positive IPO sentiment and tech strength typically lifts broad market indices including Dow tech components (MSFT, AAPL).

factors.name: Geopolitical Trade Flow Normalization
factors.summary: Strait of Hormuz reopening normalizes global trade flows and supply chains. Critical for Dow multinationals (CAT, BA, GE) reliant on uninterrupted shipping lanes. Removes major tail risk that has weighed on industrials.

factors.name: China AI Developments
factors.summary: Zhipu surged 33% as Wall Street raises China AI bets after Anthropic curbs. Mixed signal—China tech strength positive for global growth, but AI restrictions create regulatory uncertainty. Limited direct Dow impact.

factors.name: Canadian Manufacturing & Housing Data
factors.summary: Canada April manufacturing sales +4.2% vs +4.5% expected, May housing starts 261.4K vs 278.4K prior. Northern neighbor slowdown suggests broader North American industrial weakness, negative for Dow cyclicals with Canadian exposure.

confirmation.narrative: US30 positioned for continued gains as US-Iran peace framework removes major geopolitical tail risk, triggering oil price collapse (WTI <$80) and dovish repricing of Fed expectations (24 bps→16 bps hikes). Lower energy costs directly benefit Dow industrials through reduced input costs while weaker dollar (+1.3% S&P futures, Treasury yields -4 bps) supports multinational earnings translation. However, Empire Manufacturing miss (5.7 vs 14.0 expected) signals underlying industrial weakness that warrants caution—move already 1.3% underway in futures suggests Phase 2 dynamics.
catalysts_ahead: FOMC rate decision (Warsh's first meeting as Fed Chair—high uncertainty)
Bank of Japan policy decision (hike expected, yen implications for risk sentiment)
ISM Manufacturing PMI (critical after Empire Fed miss)
Formal US-Iran deal signing June 19 in Switzerland
G7 Summit in France (Trump threatened 100% wine tariffs over tech tax)
confirmation.risk_warning: Invalidation if: (1) Iran deal falls apart before June 19 signing, sending oil back above $90; (2) Empire Manufacturing weakness confirmed by broader ISM collapse, triggering recession fears; (3) Warsh's FOMC delivers unexpectedly hawkish surprise despite lower oil; (4) China data deteriorates sharply, hitting CAT/BA demand outlook. Phase 2 entry risk—futures already up 1.3%, chasing here reduces risk/reward.
confirmation.entry_guidance: Move underway with futures +1.3%—wait for cash open pullback toward 51,000-51,050 support or post-FOMC volatility shake before adding exposure rather than chasing premarket gap.

factors.name: US-Iran Deal & Geopolitical De-escalation
factors.summary: Trump announced Iran deal 'all signed'; Strait of Hormuz reopening Friday. Removes major geopolitical risk premium, reduces oil volatility, and unlocks risk-on sentiment. Companies rushing to raise $40B+ in debt on optimism.

factors.name: Oil Price Collapse
factors.summary: WTI dropped ~4.5% to $79.15 on Hormuz reopening; eases inflation pressures and input costs for Dow's transportation/logistics/industrial names (BA, CAT, UNH, CVX).

factors.name: Treasury Yields Lower
factors.summary: 10-year Treasury fell ~3bps to 4.457% on Iran deal and reduced Fed rate-hike expectations. Lower yields support valuations for Dow's dividend payers (JNJ, KO, PG) and reduce financing costs for cyclicals.

factors.name: SpaceX IPO Success & Risk Appetite
factors.summary: SpaceX raised $85.7B in record IPO; strong retail and institutional demand signals broad-based animal spirits. Boosts sentiment for tech/industrial exposure within Dow (MSFT, BA proxies).

factors.name: Dollar Weakness (Risk-On Backdrop)
factors.summary: AUD/USD rebounded 0.46%, EUR/USD near 1.1598 on risk sentiment improvement. Weaker USD typically supports multinational Dow earnings translation, though some headwind for USD-denominated energy.

factors.name: Copper & Commodities Rally
factors.summary: Copper rallies on Iran deal and Scotiabank flags supply deficit. Benefits CAT, DE, MMM—core Dow industrials dependent on commodity/infrastructure demand.

factors.name: Fed Chair Warsh Honeymoon
factors.summary: New Fed Chair Kevin Warsh receiving favorable political optics from Trump administration; June FOMC likely to hold steady. Removes near-term hawkish shock risk.

factors.name: Chip Stock Rally & Anthropic Pressure
factors.summary: Semiconductor sector surging on Iran peace and AI infrastructure demand. Anthropic export control dispute may spur broadened US AI buildout—benefits MSFT, integrated chip players.

factors.name: Price Action—Minimal Intraday Move
factors.summary: US30 only -0.04% in 4h, +0.04% in 24h despite major Iran news. Suggests market has not yet fully repriced upside or macro uncertainty remains. Move is in early innings (PHASE_2).

factors.name: Citadel Securities Warns Higher Rates & AI Skepticism
factors.summary: Macro hedge fund flags potential Fed hiking cycle and warns investors growing more discerning on AI ROI. Suggests undercurrents of caution despite today's rally.

factors.name: Fiserv CEO Departure & Sector Weakness
factors.summary: Financial services showing stress signals (71% Fiserv drop, CEO exit). Some Dow financials (GS, JPM, V) may face headwinds if confidence deteriorates.

factors.name: Data Vacuum—No Major Macro Print Last 24h
factors.summary: No CPI, PCE, NFP, or ISM data released in news window. Building permits due Tuesday and John Wiley, La-Z-Boy earnings. Limits conviction until fresh data.

screening.narrative: US30 is catching a powerful tailwind from the US-Iran peace deal signed Monday, which removes a major geopolitical risk premium, collapses oil (-4.5%), and triggers broad risk-on sentiment (AUD/USD +0.46%, EUR/USD stronger, copper rallying). Treasury yields have eased ~3bps, supporting dividend and rate-sensitive Dow names. SpaceX's record $85.7B IPO and a wave of corporate debt issuance signal strong animal spirits, while Warsh's Fed tenure begins under a political honeymoon that reduces hawkish shock risk. However, price action has been muted (US30 up only 0.04% in 24h despite the catalyst), suggesting the market is in PHASE_2—absorbing the news but not yet fully repriced. Citadel Securities' warning on higher rates and AI scrutiny adds an undercurrent of caution.
catalysts_ahead: Building permits & housing data (Tuesday)
John Wiley & Sons, La-Z-Boy earnings (Tuesday)
Iran deal signing in Geneva (Friday) — confirmation or slippage risk
FOMC meeting (mid-June window) — first under Warsh, rate guidance key
ISM Manufacturing & Services PMI (next release window)
Dow heavyweight earnings (UNH, GS, HD, CAT, V, JPM, BA)
CPI/PCE inflation print (next scheduled release)
China growth/PMI data (global demand proxy for CAT, BA, DE)
screening.risk_warning: Iran deal could collapse before Friday Geneva signing (Netanyahu political pressure, hardliner opposition); oil could reverse sharply on geopolitical flare-up. Citadel's hawkish Fed stance and AI profitability skepticism could resurface if data softens. Dow's heavy exposure to cyclicals (CAT, BA, GE, HON) means any slowdown in capex or China demand would torpedo thesis. Tariff uncertainty under Trump remains unresolved and could reignite volatility. If US30 fails to break above 52100, pullback to 51400 would suggest early momentum exhaustion.
screening.entry_guidance: Move is in PHASE_2—initial momentum from Iran deal underway but price action still muted. Consider entry on modest pullback/retest toward 51600 support before betting on break to 52100+. Avoid chasing extended moves; wait for confirmation on building permits or PMI data to sustain conviction above 52000.

confirmation.entry_guidance: Risk-on move already underway (+0.8% intraday implied); wait for pullback to 51,600-51,700 support zone before adding exposure, or breakout confirmation above 52,000.

factors.name: US-Iran Peace Deal
factors.summary: US-Iran MOU signed per Trump/Vance, Strait of Hormuz to fully reopen Friday. Major geopolitical de-escalation removes tail risk for global trade and Dow multinationals with international exposure (~50% revenue). Bloomberg reports 'investor optimism' driving risk-on positioning.

factors.name: Oil Price Collapse
factors.summary: WTI down $4.46 to $80.42 (-5.3%) on Iran deal per news feed. BEARISH for Dow component Chevron (CVX) directly, but BULLISH for industrials (CAT, BA, UPS) via lower input/fuel costs and inflation relief. Net modest positive for broad Dow basket.

factors.name: Treasury Yields Falling
factors.summary: 10-year yield down ~3bps to 4.457% per CNBC on Iran deal driving 'rethink on Fed rate hikes.' Lower yields support rate-sensitive Dow financials and dividend payers (JPM, GS, V). Eases discount rate pressure on equity valuations.

factors.name: Risk Sentiment & Equity Positioning
factors.summary: CNBC reports 'things are lining up in favor of market bulls' with oil/yields falling. S&P and Nasdaq breaking above 100-hour/200-hour MAs per feed. SpaceX IPO overhang cleared (+$10.7B greenshoe exercised). Broad risk-on tailwind lifts cyclical-heavy Dow.

factors.name: Corporate Debt Issuance Surge
factors.summary: Bloomberg reports US companies rushing to raise >$40B debt 'capitalizing on investor optimism after US-Iran deal.' Credit market confidence signal; tight spreads and strong demand indicate healthy corporate financing environment for Dow constituents.

factors.name: Fed Policy Expectations
factors.summary: Citadel Securities warns 'higher rates to challenge risk assets' and Fed 'edges toward potential rate hiking cycle.' However, immediate Iran deal impact is dovish repricing (lower yields). New Fed Chair Warsh's first meeting uncertainty caps conviction. Mixed near-term signal.

factors.name: Dollar Weakness
factors.summary: Risk-on flows weakening USD (AUD/USD +0.46%, EUR/USD holding 1.16 area). Weaker dollar benefits Dow's multinational revenue translation—~50% international exposure makes this material tailwind for Q2 earnings.

factors.name: Commodity Inputs (Copper)
factors.summary: Copper rallying on Iran deal per Bloomberg; Scotiabank lifted entire copper price deck citing 'material multi-year deficit.' Positive for industrial demand outlook (CAT, HON) but raises input cost risk. Net neutral-to-slight positive as demand signal outweighs cost.

factors.name: China/Global Growth Proxy
factors.summary: No fresh China PMI or growth data in 24h window. Iran deal removes supply chain risk (Hormuz), but no direct demand catalyst. Await upcoming China data for clearer read on CAT, BA, materials demand.

factors.name: Dow Component Specifics
factors.summary: SpaceX (not in Dow but risk gauge) rally extending. Airlines (not Dow but correlated) rallying on oil drop. CVX faces headwind from oil plunge. Salesforce ($3.6B Fin acquisition) shows tech M&A confidence. Net: cyclicals and tech outweigh energy drag in Dow weighting.

factors.name: Geopolitical Risk Premium Removal
factors.summary: BNY's Savage: 'geopolitical de-escalation has lowered energy market stress and calmed inflation concerns.' Removes war premium from equities; Dow industrials (BA, CAT) particularly sensitive to global trade disruption—now easing.

factors.name: VIX & Volatility
factors.summary: Implied by risk-on tone and equity rally; VIX likely compressing (not explicitly quoted but consistent with 'bullish mood' and corporate debt rush). Lower vol environment supports continued equity bid.

confirmation.narrative: US-Iran peace deal signed, Strait of Hormuz reopening Friday—removes major geopolitical tail risk for Dow's multinational industrials and global trade flows. Oil plunge (-5.3%) and falling yields (10Y -3bps to 4.457%) drive risk-on rotation into equities. Semiconductors and cyclicals leading; broader market 'things lining up for bulls' per CNBC. However, move already in progress (price flat 4h/24h suggests intraday rally from overnight lows), and Fed policy uncertainty (Warsh's first meeting, Citadel warns on rates) plus lack of fresh economic data cap conviction. Phase 2 setup: catalysts priced, entry selective.
catalysts_ahead: Fed Chair Warsh policy signals (first meeting under new leadership—timeline unclear but imminent focus)
Building permits data Tuesday per news
China PMI/growth data (next release)
Dow component earnings (John Wiley & Sons, La-Z-Boy Tuesday; awaiting CAT, BA, UNH, MSFT guidance)
Iran deal finalization Friday in Geneva—implementation risk remains
confirmation.risk_warning: Bias invalidates if: (1) Iran deal collapses or implementation stalls (Hormuz closure resumes), spiking oil back above $85+; (2) Fed signals imminent rate hikes (Citadel warning realized), driving 10Y yield above 4.60%; (3) Dow breaks below 51,500 support on profit-taking or energy sector contagion from oil collapse; (4) Negative surprises in upcoming ISM/China data showing demand weakness for industrials. Move already underway—chasing here risky without pullback.

factors.name: Iran Peace Deal Catalyst
factors.summary: US-Iran framework agreement signed; Strait of Hormuz set to reopen Friday. Oil down ~5% (WTI $80.75, -$4.13), reducing inflation pressures and transportation costs for industrial/consumer Dow names. Trump and markets pricing in supply normalization.

factors.name: Oil Price Collapse
factors.summary: Crude fell ~4.87% as geopolitical premium unwound. Lower energy input costs benefit industrials (CAT, BA, HON), transportation operators, and reduce headline inflation. Petrochemical prices retreating per Bloomberg.

factors.name: Risk-On Sentiment / Flight from Safety
factors.summary: S&P 500 +1.6%, Nasdaq +3.1% on the close; VIX below long-term average per MarketWatch. Equity markets globally rallying—Russia MOEX +1.07%, Mexico stocks higher. Retail piling into SpaceX (+16% day 2), signaling appetite for growth/risk.

factors.name: Inflation Peak Narrative
factors.summary: UBS and other strategists flagging May CPI as likely peak YoY; energy decline will help disinflation. Bloomberg: 'Worst of US inflation may be over if Iran peace deal sticks.' Reduces Fed pressure for sustained hawkishness.

factors.name: Fed Rate Expectations Easing
factors.summary: UBS Head of Fixed Income Leslie Falconio pushed out rate-cut call to 2027 (vs tightening pressure). Bloomberg notes pressure on Fed to hike is easing. New Fed Chair Kevin Warsh expected to hold rates steady this week; Trump has expressed trust in him.

factors.name: Dow Heavy Cyclical Positioning
factors.summary: Dow +0.9% on close; price-weighted index benefits from lower oil (CVX upside optionality), cheaper input costs (CAT, DE, MMM, HON), and risk rally in industrials/financials. Intraday hit all-time high before slight pullback.

factors.name: Treasury/Bond Repricing Risk
factors.summary: Bond market taking wait-and-see approach per Bloomberg; 10Y yield not yet decisively lower. Typically lower oil/inflation supports bond rally, but geopolitical stabilization can sometimes support real yields if growth remains intact.

factors.name: Prior Bias Consistency
factors.summary: Bias history shows BUY 6/10 → 7/10 → 6/10 over last ~12h. Fresh BUY signal aligns with Iran deal breakout; no major shift from prior reads, suggesting sustained upside posture.

factors.name: Price Action: Limited 4h/24h Decay
factors.summary: 4h change -0.48%, 24h change -0.25%. Despite massive Iran news and +1% intraday rally, market has already given back small amount. Suggests PHASE 2 (move in progress, some consolidation/profit-taking into close).

factors.name: Upcoming BOJ & RBA Decisions (Tuesday)
factors.summary: BOJ expected to hike to 1.0% (priced in); tone at post-meeting briefing key. RBA expected to hold. No direct US catalyst until Fed meeting midweek, but global rate decisions could influence USD and risk sentiment.

key_levels.resistance: 51900–52100 (intraday all-time high from Iran deal breakout; prior Dow resistance cluster)

screening.narrative: Major geopolitical de-escalation—US-Iran peace framework signed, Strait of Hormuz reopening Friday, oil collapsing ~5%—triggered broad risk-on rally and inflation-relief narrative. S&P 500 +1.6%, Nasdaq +3.1% on close; Dow +0.9%, hitting all-time intraday high. Oil input cost relief and peak-inflation expectations are bullish for Dow's industrial and financial heavyweights, while easing Fed hawkishness supports multiple expansion. However, small negative 4h and 24h price changes suggest the initial breakout move is consolidating or rolling over slightly into close—typical PHASE 2 behavior after a strong catalyst.
catalysts_ahead: BOJ rate decision & Uchida briefing (Tue ~02:30–03:30 GMT)
RBA policy decision (Tue, AEST session)
Fed Chair Kevin Warsh first meeting (FOMC decision Wed/Thu)
Actual Strait of Hormuz reopening confirmation (Fri, per Trump timeline)
June ISM Manufacturing & Services PMI (if not yet released)
Dow component earnings season acceleration (UNH, MSFT, GS, JPM, HD, BA, CAT, V, MMM updates)
China June PMI and economic data
US CPI print (if June data due mid-month)
screening.risk_warning: Iran peace deal is a framework, not finalized; details unclear (Trump said 'details to be released soon'). Geopolitical flare-up or reimposition of sanctions could reverse the move rapidly. Conversely, if bond market reprices to expect *higher* real yields (growth rally + rate-hold), equity gains could stall. Dow's 50% international revenue exposure vulnerable if USD strengthens on safe-haven reversal. Russell 2000 hit record but closed near lows of day—small-cap weakness could presage broadening pullback if risk sentiment fades into BOJ/Fed decisions.
screening.entry_guidance: Move is in PHASE 2 post-breakout; wait for pullback to 51400–51550 support before adding long exposure. If Dow reclaims 51900+ and holds through BOJ (Tue), consider stepping in for 51-52k run. Avoid chasing into event risk (FOMC Wed/Thu).

confirmation.entry_guidance: Move extended after Iran deal headline pop; wait for pullback to 51,200-51,400 or stabilization above 51,800 before adding long exposure.

factors.name: US-Iran Peace Framework
factors.summary: Trump announced Iran deal 'all signed' with Strait of Hormuz set to reopen Friday. Risk-on sentiment boosted equities broadly, though details remain unclear and implementation fragile per S&P Global analysis citing insurance constraints and infrastructure damage limiting near-term flow normalization.

factors.name: Oil Price Collapse
factors.summary: WTI crude down ~$4.13 (-4.87%) to $80.75 on Iran deal reducing supply risk. Directly bearish for CVX (Dow component) but bullish for industrial input costs and consumer spending power. Net modest positive for Dow's cyclical/manufacturing base.

factors.name: Geopolitical Risk Premium Unwind
factors.summary: VIX tumbling as traders unwind war premium. However, Netanyahu stated Israel will remain in Lebanon 'as long as needed' and explosions heard in Strait of Hormuz today per Mehr news agency, signaling deal implementation far from certain.

factors.name: Fed Rate Path Expectations
factors.summary: UBS Head of Fixed Income pushed OUT rate cut expectations to 2027 post-Iran deal, citing easing pressure on Fed to cut. Bloomberg notes worst inflation may be over if deal holds, but PGIM now forecasts THREE hikes in 2026. BOJ hiking to 1% tonight adds global hawkish tilt.

factors.name: US Dollar Strength
factors.summary: DXY lifted off 10-day lows to 99.70 despite risk-on mood. Stronger dollar headwind for Dow's ~50% international revenue exposure, creating FX translation drag on multinationals (BA, CAT, GE, MSFT).

factors.name: Treasury Yields
factors.summary: MarketWatch reports bond market taking 'wait-and-see' approach to Iran deal and Warsh's first Fed meeting. No major yield move yet; uncertainty caps both upside and downside for rate-sensitive Dow financials and dividend payers.

factors.name: Tech/AI Sector Momentum
factors.summary: Nvidia launching historic $20B bond deal, AMD flirting with $900B valuation on memory tech upgrade, chip stocks rallying hard per MarketWatch. Positive spillover to Dow tech weights (MSFT, AAPL indirectly) though Dow has lower tech concentration than SPX/NDX.

factors.name: SpaceX IPO Distraction
factors.summary: SpaceX shares +16% in second day of trading, sucking retail attention and capital. Some analysts (Rocket Lab coverage) suggest space sector rally may pull attention from traditional industrials, though Dow has minimal direct space exposure.

factors.name: Manufacturing/Industrial Data Absence
factors.summary: No major ISM, durable goods, or industrial production releases in past 24h. NAHB housing data mentioned but no specifics provided. Absence of fresh hard data leaves Dow direction dependent on macro sentiment rather than fundamentals.

factors.name: China/Global Growth Signals
factors.summary: No fresh China PMI or growth data in news window. Hormuz reopening theoretically positive for Asian supply chains (bullish CAT, BA demand) but S&P projects flows at only 75% of pre-war levels through H2 2026, limiting upside.

factors.name: Corporate-Specific Negatives
factors.summary: Defense contractors (RTX, LMT) getting contract wins but likely face reduced geopolitical premium if Iran deal holds. Fiserv CEO departure after 71% stock drop shows individual Dow component weakness. Fox acquiring Roku at $22B adds M&A uncertainty.

factors.name: Price Action Divergence
factors.summary: Russell 2000 hit record but closed near session lows despite oil collapse benefiting small-caps most. investingLive notes 'not the best finish to the day' - late-session fade suggests profit-taking and skepticism about Iran deal durability.

confirmation.narrative: Dow rallied ~1% intraday to new ATH on headline-driven risk-on following US-Iran peace framework announcement, but closed well off highs amid implementation doubts and hawkish Fed repricing. Oil collapse is two-edged sword: helps industrial input costs but pressures CVX and signals deal skepticism. Multiple credible sources (S&P Global, Kpler, insurance underwriters) warn Hormuz normalization will be slow and partial through 2026. Late-session fade in Russell 2000 and continued explosions in Hormuz per Iranian media suggest markets bought the headline without scrutinizing fine print. Bias history shows sustained BUY conviction from prior reads, but current pop may be Phase 3 extension rather than fresh opportunity.
catalysts_ahead: Fed decision with new Chair Warsh (this week) - critical for rate path repricing
BOJ rate hike to 1% (tonight ~0230-0330 GMT) - yen strength could pressure US equities
Friday Iran deal MOU signing per S&P Global - implementation risk event
Any ISM Manufacturing or Services PMI (next scheduled releases)
China PMI/growth data (monthly cycle)
Dow component earnings (no specific dates in news but UNH, GS, CAT, BA guidance windows approaching)
confirmation.risk_warning: Deal implementation failure (Netanyahu already signaling non-compliance on Lebanon withdrawal, explosions continuing in Hormuz today) would reverse entire risk-on trade violently. Fed hiking 3x in 2026 per PGIM would crater rate-sensitive Dow components. Stronger dollar at 99.70+ creates persistent FX headwind. Late-day fade suggests institutional skepticism - if Dow fails to hold 51,400 support, signals headline pop was short-squeeze rather than conviction buying.
